title: eragrostis coromandeliana
intro: Eragrostis coromandeliana, also known as the Indian grass or Coromandel grass, is a perennial grass native to India and other parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, Eragrostis coromandeliana is often used as a tea or decoction, and it can also be used in the form of powder or extract, which can be further processed into capsules or tablets.
